parts.prompt:
Find the rate of change of r with respect to i at the point on curve C where i = 1.3 . Show the
setup for your calculations.

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (a)

parts.prompt:
Find the area of the region that lies inside curve C but outside the graph of the polar equation
r = 2. Show the setup for your calculations.

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (b)

parts.prompt:
It can be shown that dx = 4sinicos2 i - 2sin3 i for curve C. For 0 # i # r 2 , find the value
di
of i that corresponds to the point on curve C that is farthest from the y-axis. Justify your
answer.

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (c)

parts.prompt:
A particle travels along curve C so that
di
= 15 for all times t. Find the rate at which the
dt
particle’s distance from the origin changes with respect to time when the particle is at the
point where i = 1.3 . Show the setup for your calculations.
